markdown.markdown:
My research sits at the intersection of **systems and control theory**, **statistical signal processing**,
**machine learning**, and **robotics**. A common thread is developing principled mathematical methods
that enable autonomous systems to reason, learn, and act intelligently in uncertain dynamic environments.

---

### Inverse Optimal Control & Learning from Demonstrations

I develop theory for inferring cost functions and rational strategies directly from observed behaviour —
a field spanning *inverse optimal control* (single-agent) and *inverse game theory* (multi-agent).
These methods allow autonomous systems to learn objectives from demonstrations without hand-crafted
reward engineering, and have been applied to modelling bird mid-air collision avoidance, human–robot
interaction, and aircraft separation assurance.

This work culminated in an authored Springer Nature textbook and contributions to
*Automatica*, *IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control*, and major IFAC/CDC venues.

---

### Partially Observed Markov Decision Processes & Active State Estimation

I have developed entropy-based methods for active state estimation and trajectory obfuscation in
partially observed Markov decision processes (POMDPs), finding new tractable solutions to problems
previously considered intractable in robotics and AI. Applications include autonomous robot exploration,
privacy preservation, and cybersecurity. Work in this theme has appeared in
*IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control* and *IEEE Control Systems Letters*.

---

### Minimax Robust Quickest Change Detection

I have made fundamental contributions to minimax robust quickest change detection — the problem of
detecting an abrupt change in a stochastic process as quickly as possible while bounding false-alarm
rates under unknown or misspecified models. Methods have been applied to vision-based aircraft
detection and tracking for autonomous systems. Key publications appear in
*IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing*, *IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control*, and *Automatica*.

---

### Multi-Agent Dynamic and Differential Game Theory

I investigate differential and dynamic game theory for multi-agent systems, including solution
concepts and performance guarantees under partial information. Recent work explores human–machine
teaming and adversarial pursuit–evasion problems. This theme is supported by US Air Force Research
Laboratory (AOARD) funding and recent ARC Discovery Project grants.

---

### Robot Perception & Event Cameras

I develop novel algorithms for event camera-based robot perception, including asynchronous object
tracking and spatio-temporal event processing. This work is supported by ARC Discovery Project
DP250100112 (*Seeing through Space and Time: Spatio-Temporal Event Processing for Robots*)
in collaboration with Prof Robert Mahony and Prof Girish Nair. Key publications appear in
*IEEE Transactions on Robotics* and *IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters*.

---

## Supervision

I have over AUD$2.2M in research funding and supervise students across robotics, control, and signal processing.
